# Chapter 5: Programming languages and paradigms  (~120 pp)
- 5.1 Imperative, functional, declarative paradigms (15) · 5.2 Type systems (18) · 5.3 Memory management: manual, RAII, GC, ownership (18) · 5.4 Concurrency primitives at the language level (15) · 5.5 Metaprogramming and macros (12) · 5.6 Comparing C, Python, Rust, Lisp, Haskell engineering-first (20) · 5.7 Failure section: type errors, null-pointer dereferences, the undefined-behaviour cost (22)

**Archetype**: interface, failure. **Project**: implement a small interpreter in two languages; reflect on what each forced into the design. **Exercises**: 35.
